item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel this really captures the character-focused nature of GAW and uses the group's chat dynamic in an excellent way, that manages to be quite emotional.

This is the kind of article that shows how GAW can shine. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok. Good point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like how this tonally handles an online dispute from multiple angles and the difficulty of collaborating on an emotionally charged project and I also really like that #FaithBassenWasMurdered is basically a modern day version of WHO PUT BELLA IN THE WYTCH ELM and conveys that same kind of anger in a fictional context. I think that's fucking good. I think that's extremely fucking good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: bluntfiend is old he doesn't know shit. are they going to criticize their ostensible leader for being old and behind the times? (Yes, but not in this context.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not far fetched that a close friend group would often use old memes well past their general "expiration date", either out of nostalgia, irony, inside jokes, or just for fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
lesbian_gengar: hey whoah let's not throw shade at the dead
bluntfiend: Lest the dead dab back.

Dabs aren't a good meme anymore now and I doubt that they will be in 2020. I fell like this should be changed or result in about one GAW member chastising bluntfiend. GAW does know their memes after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"FAITH BASSEN WAS MURDERED"

This line might just make a better title. Or maybe we could do something like "4chamber Faith Bassen was murdered."

What do you think?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't get behind this skip. Story behind it really could use more effort put into it to try and get the reader to actually care about the characters' situation, but as it stands right now it just gives off the vibe of forcing in its themes for a cheap way to get sympathy without needing to actually develop anyone. Skips like this need more than what's presented here if you want to tell an emotional story.

The chat logs are unrealistically dramatic, and create a big problem with whatever immersion the article could muster, it doesn't feel like real people are messaging each other.

Not to mention that the safe classification makes no sense for a skip that could appear whenever a pretty common post layout is posted online, which would at the very least make it euclid on the basis of uncertainty for whether or not it could spike in activity again.

-1. It's not totally unsalvageable, but it'll be difficult to make it good without rewriting the whole thing.

item.title: Re: MOD POST - INFO - OPEN
item.content:encoded: Ok, sorry.

item.title: MOD POST - INFO - OPEN
item.content:encoded:
Don't try and backseat mod please.

The title is fine. Leave staff work to staff in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wouldn't be surprised if a Mod or Administrator told you to change the title.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This really subverted my expectations. Seeing the title I was expecting something humorous, but the more I read into it the reality became clear. :(

+1, a good skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why is this sad? Quavo shows up and days “Glah” before leaving.

Comedy Gold

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've already talked to PG about this on IRC, but if you can think of a better title for this I'm all ears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this was really beautiful, im sorry she didn't get the justice she deserved

thought it would be a funny one from the title, was a bit of a shock tbh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The first paragraph of the description is dense as all hell. I read it like three times and I'm still unsure what precisely happens. Please tighten that up.

The rest is good, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with you, yeah - I'll be changing that once the competition is over

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted this because, damn.

One note, this:

The primary parameter ascertained is that the content is not owned or created by the poster, and a caption of or within the content alludes to a desire to discover the content's creator, whether or not said desire can be judged to be genuine

Is really awkward to read. I got the concept eventually, but it definitely got mangled in the conversion to clinical/formal tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second this sentiment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love that title, and I love this skip, but I really, really do not love them together. I feel like there was a better title for this skip and a better skip for this title.

I'm still upvoting, of course, but I just wanted to get that off my chest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
>:'(

I saw the title and thought it'd be funny. Take this +1, you meanie!!!

>:'(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also check out my submission for day two of the writing jam, SCP-3224

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i did this
